factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, supported by strong operating cash flows and efficient capital expenditures.

evidence.text: Cash flows provided by operating activities have historically supplied us with a significant source of liquidity. [...] Net cash provided by operating activities was $298.2 million for the three months ended August 31, 2022, compared to $262.1 million for the three months ended August 31, 2021. [...] Capital expenditures were $70.0 million and $48.7 million for the three months ended August 31, 2022 and 2021, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

factors.summary: The company is increasing capital expenditures to support growth and market penetration.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures were $70.0 million and $48.7 million for the three months ended August 31, 2022 and 2021, respectively. [...] The increase in capital expenditures during the three months ended August 31, 2022 over the same period in the prior fiscal year is due to an investment in the operating segments to support continued market penetration and revenue growth.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

factors.summary: The company faces ordinary course legal proceedings, but no material adverse effect is expected.

evidence.text: Cintas is subject to other legal proceedings, insurance receipts, legal settlements and claims arising from the ordinary course of its business, including personal injury, customer contract, environmental and employment claims. In the opinion of management, the aggregate liability, if any, with respect to such ordinary course of business actions will not have a material adverse effect on the consolidated financial position, consolidated results of operations or consolidated cash flows of Cintas.
evidence.subsection: Litigation and Other Contingencies

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, supported by new business growth and increased penetration.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 14.2% to $2,166.5 million for the three months ended August 31, 2022, compared to $1,897.0 million for the three months ended August 31, 2021. The organic revenue growth rate, which adjusts for the impact of acquisitions, divestitures and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, was 13.9%. Revenue growth was positively impacted by 0.5% due primarily to acquisitions and negatively impacted by 0.2% due to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, benefiting overall performance.

evidence.text: Other revenue, consisting of revenue from the First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment and All Other, increased 20.6% for the three months ended August 31, 2022, compared to the same period in the prior fiscal year, from $388.8 million to $468.7 million. The organic revenue growth rate for other revenue was 20.6%. Revenue growth was positively impacted by 0.1% due primarily to acquisitions and negatively impacted by 0.1% due to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by strong performance across segments.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth rate, which adjusts for the impact of acquisitions, divestitures and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, was 13.9%. Revenue growth was positively impacted by 0.5% due primarily to acquisitions and negatively impacted by 0.2% due to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Cost of uniform rental and facility services increased $111.5 million, or 14.3%, for the three months ended August 31, 2022, compared to the three months ended August 31, 2021. This change from the same period in the prior fiscal year was primarily due to higher Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment sales volume, as well as increased energy costs and investments in labor and material cost to support increased revenue growth achieved during the three months ended August 31, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: The First Aid and Safety Services segment is growing fastest, driven by new business and product penetration.

evidence.text: First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment revenue increased from $199.1 million to $234.2 million, or 17.6%, for the three months ended August 31, 2022, over the same period in the prior fiscal year. The organic revenue growth rate for the reportable operating segment was 15.8%. First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment revenue was positively impacted by 1.8% due to acquisitions. The increase in revenue was driven by many factors including new business sold by sales representatives, penetration of additional products and services into existing customers, price increases and strong customer retention.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly energy costs, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: Cost of uniform rental and facility services increased $111.5 million, or 14.3%, for the three months ended August 31, 2022, compared to the three months ended August 31, 2021. This change from the same period in the prior fiscal year was primarily due to higher Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment sales volume, as well as increased energy costs and investments in labor and material cost to support increased revenue growth achieved during the three months ended August 31, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to higher expenses.

evidence.text: Selling and administrative expenses increased $79.3 million, or 15.6%, in the three months ended August 31, 2022, compared to the same period of the prior fiscal year. In the three months ended August 31, 2021, there was a gain on the sale of certain operating assets within the Uniform Direct Sales operating segment of $12.2 million, which was recorded as a reduction of selling and administrative expenses. The remaining increase of $67.2 million, or 12.9%, was primarily due to increases in labor and other employee-partner expenses.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to rising costs and labor expenses.

evidence.text: Operating income was $440.1 million, or 20.3% of revenue, for the three months ended August 31, 2022, compared to $394.1 million, or 20.8% of revenue, for the three months ended August 31, 2021. The decrease in operating income as a percent of revenue was due to a gain on the sale of certain operating assets within the Uniform Direct Sales operating segment of $12.2 million, or 70 basis points, recorded in the three months ended August 31, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by labor and delivery route efficiencies.

evidence.text: Cost of other improved as a percentage of revenue, decreasing from 55.3% for three months ended August 31, 2021 to 52.8% for the three months ended August 31, 2022. The improvement in cost of sales as a percent to revenue was primarily due to favorable changes in the sales mix for each of the underlying operating segments as well as efficiencies gained in labor and delivery routes, partially offset by increases in energy costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: Management appears confident, as evidenced by continued investment in labor and materials to support growth.

evidence.text: Cintas’ principal objective is “to exceed customers’ expectations in order to maximize the long-term value of Cintas for shareholders and working partners,” and it provides the framework and focus for Cintas’ business strategy. This strategy is to achieve revenue growth for all our products and services by increasing our penetration at existing customers and by broadening our customer base to include market segments to which we have not historically served. We will also continue to identify additional product and service opportunities for our current and future customers.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is clear and focused on revenue growth through customer penetration and market expansion.

evidence.text: Cintas’ principal objective is “to exceed customers’ expectations in order to maximize the long-term value of Cintas for shareholders and working partners,” and it provides the framework and focus for Cintas’ business strategy. This strategy is to achieve revenue growth for all our products and services by increasing our penetration at existing customers and by broadening our customer base to include market segments to which we have not historically served. We will also continue to identify additional product and service opportunities for our current and future customers.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to energy costs and labor expenses, which are impacting margins.

evidence.text: The change in gross margin was caused by a 40 basis point increase in energy-related expenses and investments in labor and material cost to support increased revenue growth achieved, partially offset by improved leverage of fixed costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: Higher interest rates are increasing the company's net interest expense, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: Net interest expense (interest expense less interest income) was $27.6 million for the three months ended August 31, 2022, compared to $21.8 million for the three months ended August 31, 2021. The change was primarily due to an increase in interest rates on commercial paper and an increase in outstanding short-term debt during the three months ended August 31, 2022 compared to the three months ended August 31, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: Foreign exchange fluctuations are negatively impacting revenue growth.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 14.2% to $2,166.5 million for the three months ended August 31, 2022, compared to $1,897.0 million for the three months ended August 31, 2021. The organic revenue growth rate, which adjusts for the impact of acquisitions, divestitures and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, was 13.9%. Revenue growth was positively impacted by 0.5% due primarily to acquisitions and negatively impacted by 0.2% due to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: The company is actively engaged in strategic acquisitions to drive growth and expand its customer base, with recent activities concentrated in key operating segments. The evidence shows consistent and increasing investment in acquisitions, particularly in the Uniform Rental and Facility Services, First Aid and Safety Services, and Fire Protection segments.

evidence.text: Cash paid for acquisitions of businesses was $7.1 million and $35.7 million for the three months ended August 31, 2022 and 2021, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: We pursue the strategy of broadening our customer base in several ways... We also broaden our customer base through geographic expansion. Finally, we evaluate strategic acquisitions as opportunities arise.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: The company is consistently increasing shareholder returns through share buybacks and maintaining a dividend policy, signaling strong management confidence.

evidence.text: From the inception of the July 27, 2021 share buyback program through August 31, 2022, Cintas purchased a total of 2.7 million shares of Cintas common stock at an average price of $385.66 per share for a total purchase price of $1.0 billion.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: The Board of Directors declared dividends, with a focus on maintaining a consistent dividend policy.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

evidence.text: The decrease in diluted weighted average common shares outstanding resulted from purchasing an aggregate of approximately 2.7 million shares of common stock under the board approved share buyback programs since the beginning of the third quarter of fiscal 2022 through the first quarter of fiscal 2023.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is stable, with a decrease in cash used in financing activities primarily due to reduced share buyback activity and debt payments, partially offset by decreased net issuance of commercial paper. Net interest expense has increased due to higher interest rates on commercial paper and increased outstanding short-term debt.

evidence.text: 'Net cash used in financing activities was $226.2 million and $590.1 million for the three months ended August 31, 2022 and 2021, respectively. The decrease in cash used in financing activities was primarily due to the decrease in share buyback activity and debt payments, partially offset by the decrease in net issuance of commercial paper in the three months ended August 31, 2022.'
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: 'Net interest expense (interest expense less interest income) was $27.6 million for the three months ended August 31, 2022, compared to $21.8 million for the three months ended August 31, 2021. The change was primarily due to an increase in interest rates on commercial paper and an increase in outstanding short-term debt during the three months ended August 31, 2022 compared to the three months ended August 31, 2021.'
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended August 31, 2022 Compared to Three Months Ended August 31, 2021
